issuesFound.title: HTML element missing lang attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <html> element lacks a lang attribute, which prevents screen readers from correctly pronouncing content in the appropriate language.

issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu button contains only an aria-hidden span, providing no accessible name for screen reader users to identify the control's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 41: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Link text non-descriptive ("Read more")
issuesFound.explanation: The visible link text "Read more" provides no context about the destination when read out of context by screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "Feature" header cell lacks a scope attribute in an ambiguous table with multiple headers, preventing screen readers from correctly associating the header with its column.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button controls the expandable nav menu but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the menu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 41: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation menu">

issuesFound.explanation: Multiple <nav> elements exist on the page; this instance lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation regions when navigating by landmarks.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for high-contrast mode users. Focus becomes completely invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the focus ring from buttons. No box-shadow or border replacement is provided in this rule.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px without sufficient padding to reach the recommended 44px target size, potentially making them difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it in the accessibility tree. It also lacks clip properties. Screen reader users will not hear this content, and sighted users will see it.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: Search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient padding compensation to reach the recommended 44px target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. This creates a too-small touch target for users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA primary button
issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA primary button has transition including transform and hover translateY movement without a reduced-motion media query override.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on form checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: Form checkboxes have width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Global rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links. While some specific links have hover underlines, footer navigation links (line 1076-1080) rely solely on color change for differentiation, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 191: 
  ```
  a { text-decoration: underline; }
  ```

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation toggle removes outline and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove outline and only set negative outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card link focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline and only set outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline and only set outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The table wrapper focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, making it invisible when focused via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline and only set negative outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkbox:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form checkboxes remove outline and only set outline-offset, leaving no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary text
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-primary variable is set to #555555 (line 20) and applied to body text (line 159) against a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.0:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 20: 
  ```
  --colour-text-primary:     var(--colour-neutral-800);
  ```

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove outline and only set outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove outline and only set outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove outline and only set outline-offset, leaving no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on header logo link:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Header logo link removes outline and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: Small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Button transitions include transform properties that cause spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: Button active state uses transform: translateY(1px) for spatial movement without a reduced-motion override, which may affect users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: Hero image has transition and hover transform (perspective rotation) causing spatial movement, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 584 is empty and fails to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards have transition including transform and hover state with translateY movement, but no reduced-motion override is provided for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards have transition including transform and hover translateY movement without a reduced-motion override, potentially affecting users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: Body text has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa (line 44) and used for secondary text against white backgrounds. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.46:1, failing WCAG AA requirements for text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 44: 
  ```
  --colour-text-secondary:   var(--colour-neutral-600);
  ```

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users will not see where focus is located.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block for :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Focus will be invisible in high-contrast mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users interacting with buttons will lose focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform transition without a valid prefers-reduced-motion override (the block at Line 403 is empty). Users who prefer reduced motion will still see animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 403: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transform transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p text colour #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 315: .section-header p { color: #64748b; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-tagline text colour #5588bb on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 915: .product-tagline { color: #003888; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label text colour rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a dark gradient background has very low contrast, likely below 3:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: .stat-label { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand > p
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p text colour #94a3b8 on background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: .footer-brand > p { color: #cbd5e1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links text colour #94a3b8 on background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1235: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { color: #cbd5e1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p text colour #64748b on background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.3:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: .footer-bottom > p { color: #cbd5e1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a text colour #94a3b8 on background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250: .social-links a { color: #cbd5e1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint text colour #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1290: .input-hint { color: #64748b; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .required-marker
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-marker text colour #ee9999 on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which is below the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: .required-marker { color: #991b1b; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links have text-decoration: none and hover state only changes color. Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1235: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The social media links have text-decoration: none and hover state only changes color. Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1250: .social-links a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links have text-decoration: none and hover state only changes color. Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 635: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields fail validation, the code updates error messages and CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input. Valid fields also do not clear aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line _renderResult(): add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', String(!result.valid));` before the if/else block, or add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the else block and `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` in the if block.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers toggle the visibility of their associated panels but do not update aria-expanded to reflect the open/closed state. Assistive technologies cannot announce the current state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line _closeInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology receive no confirmation that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.title));` before the emit call
  Line close(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title));` before the emit call

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are initialized with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" on page load when the panel is initially hidden. Screen readers may assume the dropdown is already expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line register(): add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters and the product grid updates, the result count change is not announced. Screen reader users are unaware that filtering completed or how many results are visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line applyFilters(): add after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`:
  `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.filterApplied || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons toggle active CSS classes but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate the selected state. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active in the tab group.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _syncTabAriaStates(): replace the forEach body with:
  `const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not initialize or update aria-expanded to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is open or closed, violating the ARIA combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mount(): add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting aria-haspopup
  Line show(): add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` 
  Line hide(): add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period switch not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates prices visually but provides no audible feedback to screen reader users about the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line setPeriod(): add after `_syncToggleState(period);`:
  `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) visually indicate the active period but do not update aria-pressed, failing to communicate the pressed state to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _syncToggleState(): replace the forEach body with:
  `const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing switch role missing aria-checked updates
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period control is not a native button, it is assigned role="switch" but the aria-checked attribute is never updated to reflect the current state (month vs annual).
issuesFound.fix:
Line _syncToggleState(): add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(period === 'year'));` 
  Line _onToggleClick(): ensure the aria-checked update happens via setPeriod or add directly:
  `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: filterAnimation opacity-only hiding leaves elements accessible
issuesFound.explanation: During the fade-out animation, product cards are set to opacity:0 but remain in the accessibility tree and can receive keyboard focus for the duration of the animation. Screen reader and keyboard users may encounter invisible focused elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line Phase 1 fade out: add `el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` inside the toHide.forEach block alongside the opacity transition, or add `el.style.visibility = 'hidden';` after the fade completes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function toggles the navigation drawer visibility by adding the `is-open` class but fails to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function hides the navigation drawer but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. This leaves the accessibility state out of sync with the visual state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger button. Assistive technologies may announce the menu state incorrectly on page load.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. The accessibility state remains "expanded" visually.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.init` function discovers triggers but does not initialize their `aria-expanded` attribute to "false". Initial state is undefined for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch._open` function shows the suggestion panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the search input (combobox pattern). Screen readers do not announce the list is visible.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch._close` function hides the suggestion panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the search input. The combobox state remains incorrectly marked as expanded.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch.init` function mounts the listbox but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the input element.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. Users are not informed the answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.close` function collapses the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. The state remains "expanded" for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._syncToggleState` function updates the visual active state of billing period buttons but does not update `aria-pressed`. Users cannot identify the selected period via screen reader.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not remove aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid` function removes the error description but does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. The field remains marked as invalid in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _updateCountBadge not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._updateCountBadge` function updates the product count text dynamically but the element is not an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users are not notified of the count change.

issuesFound.title: pricing _updateSavingsMessage not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._updateSavingsMessage` function updates the savings text dynamically without an `aria-live` region. Users are not informed of the new discount information.

fpTitles: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded
dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
dropdownMenu init does not initialize aria-expanded
siteSearch _close function does not update aria-expanded
siteSearch init does not initialize aria-expanded
faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded

pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed
srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
productFilter _updateCountBadge not announced via live region
pricing _updateSavingsMessage not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop to style error states but does not map it to aria-invalid, preventing assistive technology from recognizing invalid field states.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} to the input: `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button expands and collapses a sub-menu section but does not expose its state via aria-expanded, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current expansion state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: Individual star characters are rendered as text content without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce each star separately instead of the composite rating.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true" to the inner span: `<span key={i} className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'} aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not excluded from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: Slides that are not currently visible lack aria-hidden="true", allowing screen reader users to navigate to and read content from hidden slides.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible}: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ question button toggles the answer panel but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the answer is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links container is a nav element without an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Skip navigation": `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation">`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not expose it as aria-invalid to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}: `<textarea id={id} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button controls the MobileNav panel but does not communicate its open/closed state via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page navigation link is visually indicated but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in mobile navigation lacks aria-current="page".
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks an accessible name via aria-label or aria-labelledby.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading": `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only the character "‹" and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial": `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the character "›" and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Next testimonial": `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: StarRating widget missing role="img"
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating container has an aria-label describing the rating value but lacks role="img", preventing screen readers from treating it as a single image entity.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add role="img": `<span role="img" aria-label={`${labelPrefix}: ${value} out of ${max} stars`}>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker announces asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" or "asterisk" redundantly alongside the "required" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true": ` *`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The button enters a loading state but does not communicate this busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-busy={isLoading}: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button contains only the character "✕" and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu": `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">`

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not expose it as aria-invalid to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}: `<select id={id} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} {...rest} >`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when an error exists, but FormGroup is not provided an errorId prop, so no element with that ID is rendered. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-name-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not receive an errorId prop, so the error message lacks this ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not receive an errorId prop.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button shows an active state but lacks aria-pressed to communicate whether monthly billing is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button shows an active state but lacks aria-pressed to communicate whether annual billing is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button toggles video playback state but uses only a dynamic aria-label. It should use aria-pressed to indicate the current playing state for better toggle button semantics.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={isPlaying}: `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation lacks an accessible name to distinguish it from other nav elements on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Main": `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a nav element without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Mobile": `<nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: StatsBar region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section has a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section lacks aria-labelledby to expose that heading as the region's name.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading": `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card article contains a heading with id={headingId} but lacks aria-labelledby to expose the plan name to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby={headingId}: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for visual marquee scrolling is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true": `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div not keyboard accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the menu but is not focusable and lacks keyboard event handlers, making the close action unavailable to keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div not keyboard accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The modal backdrop has an onClick handler to close the dialog but lacks keyboard support (cannot be focused or activated with Enter/Space).
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add role="button", tabIndex={-1} (to allow programmatic focus but not tab order), and onKeyDown: `<div className="modal-overlay" role="button" tabIndex={-1} aria-label="Close dialog" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} aria-modal="true">`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron character indicating expand/collapse state is decorative but not marked with aria-hidden, causing redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden="true": `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element with role="dialog", potentially causing assistive technology to incorrectly identify the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Move aria-modal="true" to the dialog div and remove from overlay: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>` and `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping skip links is a landmark region but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav id="main-nav"> element is a landmark region but lacks an aria-label. Since multiple <nav> elements exist on the page (mobile, footer), each requires a unique accessible name to be distinguishable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section className="hero-section"> element is a major page region but lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby. This prevents screen reader users from identifying the section's purpose when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 635: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: Stats section landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section className="stats-bar"> element contains a heading (h2#stats-heading) but the section itself lacks aria-labelledby referencing it. This breaks the landmark association for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 705: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation submenu trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggling the mobile submenu visibility (via isExpanded state) lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users are not informed of the submenu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggling the mobile navigation panel (via isOpen state) lacks aria-expanded. While aria-label changes text, the standard state property for disclosure widgets is missing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 575: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component renders an icon-only button but the parent ThemeToggle does not pass the required aria-label prop. The button has no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 625: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the previous slide control contains only a chevron character and lacks an aria-label prop. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the next slide control contains only a chevron character and lacks an aria-label prop. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1341: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: Modal close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the modal dialog contains only an '×' character and lacks an aria-label prop. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: <IconButton aria-label="Close dialog" className="modal-dialog__close" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Hero video toggle icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component in HeroSection renders an icon span inside a button that already has an aria-label. The icon is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true" because srLabel prop is not passed to the Button component.
issuesFound.fix: Line 665: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} srLabel="Toggle background video" onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply the aria-invalid attribute to the underlying <input> element. Error states are not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply the aria-invalid attribute to the underlying <textarea> element. Error states are not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy attribute for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not apply aria-busy to the underlying <button> element. Assistive technology users are not informed that the button is busy processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page (via item.current) uses a class for styling but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed this is the active page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 448: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page (via item.current) uses a class for styling but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed this is the active page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> element representing a pricing plan lacks aria-labelledby referencing its internal heading (h3#headingId). Screen reader users navigating by landmarks/articles cannot identify the plan name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1167: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces asterisk to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "asterisk" unnecessarily, while the "required" state is already conveyed via aria-required/required on the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: *

issuesFound.title: Integrations bar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The cloned <ul> element used for visual marquee looping is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen reader users will encounter duplicate content for every integration logo.
issuesFound.fix: Line 795: <ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">

issuesFound.title: Star rating individual stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans inside the StarRating component are decorative but lack aria-hidden="true". The container has an aria-label, so the internal stars should be hidden to prevent redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: <span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'}>
